electric-charges-fields.subject: physics
electric-charges-fields.title: Electrostatics
syllabusOutline: Electric charges, conservation of charge, Coulomb's law force between two point charges.
Electric field, electric field due to a point charge, electric dipole, electric field due to a dipole.
Gauss's law statement and its applications to find field due to infinitely long straight wire, uniformly charged infinite plane sheet.
Electric potential, potential difference, electric potential due to a point charge, potential energy of a system of charges.
Capacitors and capacitance, combination of capacitors in series and in parallel, capacitance of a parallel plate capacitor.
electric-charges-fields.studySummary:
## Syllabus outline
- Electric charges, conservation of charge, Coulomb's law force between two point charges.
- Electric field, electric field due to a point charge, electric dipole, electric field due to a dipole.
- Gauss's law statement and its applications to find field due to infinitely long straight wire, uniformly charged infinite plane sheet.
- Electric potential, potential difference, electric potential due to a point charge, potential energy of a system of charges.
- Capacitors and capacitance, combination of capacitors in series and in parallel, capacitance of a parallel plate capacitor.

Board-oriented study guide for CBSE Class 11 Physics — Electrostatics.

videos.title: Electric Charges And Fields One Shot | Physics | Class 12th Boards

ray-optics-instruments.subject: physics
ray-optics-instruments.title: Ray Optics and Optical Instruments
syllabusOutline: Refraction of light, total internal reflection and its applications, optical fibers.
Refraction at spherical surfaces, lenses, thin lens formula, lens maker's formula.
Magnification, power of a lens, combination of thin lenses in contact.
Refraction of light through a prism, derivation of prism formula.
Optical instruments: Microscopes and astronomical telescopes (reflecting and refracting).
ray-optics-instruments.studySummary:
## Syllabus outline
- Refraction of light, total internal reflection and its applications, optical fibers.
- Refraction at spherical surfaces, lenses, thin lens formula, lens maker's formula.
- Magnification, power of a lens, combination of thin lenses in contact.
- Refraction of light through a prism, derivation of prism formula.
- Optical instruments: Microscopes and astronomical telescopes (reflecting and refracting).

chemical-kinetics.subject: chemistry
chemical-kinetics.title: Chemical Kinetics
syllabusOutline: Rate of a reaction (average and instantaneous), factors affecting rates of reaction.
Rate law, specific rate constant, order and molecularity of a reaction.
Integrated rate equations and half-life (only for zero and first order reactions).
Concept of collision theory (elementary idea, no mathematical treatment).
chemical-kinetics.studySummary:
## Syllabus outline
- Rate of a reaction (average and instantaneous), factors affecting rates of reaction.
- Rate law, specific rate constant, order and molecularity of a reaction.
- Integrated rate equations and half-life (only for zero and first order reactions).
- Concept of collision theory (elementary idea, no mathematical treatment).
- Activation energy, Arrhenius equation.

Board-oriented study guide for CBSE Class 11 Chemistry — Chemical Kinetics.

videos.title: Chemical Kinetics One Shot Revision | Class 12 Chemistry

coordination-compounds.subject: chemistry
coordination-compounds.title: Coordination Compounds
syllabusOutline: Coordination compounds - Introduction, ligands, coordination number, color, magnetic properties.
Werner's theory of coordination compounds, IUPAC nomenclature of mononuclear coordination compounds.
Isomerism (structural and stereo) in coordination compounds.
Valence Bond Theory (VBT): hybridization, magnetic behavior (high spin and low spin).
Crystal Field Theory (CFT): d-orbital splitting in octahedral and tetrahedral fields, CFSE.
coordination-compounds.studySummary:
## Syllabus outline
- Coordination compounds - Introduction, ligands, coordination number, color, magnetic properties.
- Werner's theory of coordination compounds, IUPAC nomenclature of mononuclear coordination compounds.
- Isomerism (structural and stereo) in coordination compounds.
- Valence Bond Theory (VBT): hybridization, magnetic behavior (high spin and low spin).
- Crystal Field Theory (CFT): d-orbital splitting in octahedral and tetrahedral fields, CFSE.

Board-oriented study guide for CBSE Class 11 Chemistry — Coordination Compounds.
